articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Piero Hincapié replaces Martin Terrier because of an injury.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0. Florian Wirtz (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Granit Xhaka following a fast break.
First Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0.
Tomás Cvancara (Borussia Mönchengladbach) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0. Florian Wirtz (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Jeremie Frimpong replaces Nathan Tella.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Florian Wirtz with a through ball.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Kevin Stöger replaces Robin Hack.

Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Alassane Pléa replaces Tomás Cvancara.

Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Shio Fukuda replaces Philipp Sander.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3, Borussia Mönchengladbach 1. Tim Kleindienst (Borussia Mönchengladbach) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Luca Netz with a cross.
Stefan Lainer (Borussia Mönchengladbach) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

article.headline: Wirtz scores twice in Leverkusen's 3-1 win over Gladbach
article.description: Bayer Leverkusen's Florian Wirtz scored twice and set up another goal as he steered the German champions to a comfortable 3-1 victory over visiting Borussia Moenchengladbach on Saturday to stay four points behind leaders Bayern Munich.

article.story:
Bayer Leverkusen's Florian Wirtz scored twice and set up another goal as he steered the German champions to a comfortable 3-1 victory over visiting Borussia Moenchengladbach on Saturday to stay four points behind leaders Bayern Munich.

Last season's domestic double winners have now won 11 straight matches across all competitions ahead of Tuesday's Champions League match at Atletico Madrid.

"It was tough to get this result. But we had good control and patience and we played a mature game," coach Xabi Alonso told a press conference.

"I am very satisfied with his (Wirtz's) performance. He had a top, top impact on the game. His mentality is top. He always wants to play, to win and have fun on the pitch and you can see that," the coach added.

Leverkusen suffered an early setback when forward Martin Terrier had to be taken off with an ankle injury after nine minutes.

"It does not look good with Martin," Alonso said. "It looks like a heel injury. We will wait for tomorrow to see but it is a pity for Martin. Tomorrow we will have a clearer situation."

The hosts found it tough going against a disciplined Gladbach backline until Wirtz decided to take matters into his own hands.

The Germany international picked up a pass from Granit Xhaka well outside the box, then superbly shook off Ko Itakura before slotting home in the 32nd minute.

The 21-year-old doubled the lead with a well-taken 62nd- minute penalty to take his tally to nine league goals, and turned provider 12 minutes later, setting up Patrik Schick for the Czech's 12th league goal.

Gladbach grabbed a consolation in stoppage time through Tim Kleindienst.

Leverkusen are in second place on 41 points with Bayern in top spot on 45 following their 3-2 win over VfL Wolfsburg.
